seed_problem: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.
mutation: input: relax the conflict graph from bipartite to a general (Erdos-Renyi + hub-augmented) graph so maximum-weight independent set becomes NP-hard, with weights correlated to degree so weight-greedy and degree-discounted heuristics diverge.
title: Salmon Ladder Watch: Non-Interfering Counting Weir Placement
strategies: trivial baseline: activate only the single highest-value site (the checker's baseline B)
weight-greedy: sort sites by data value descending, add each if it conflicts with nothing already chosen
degree-discounted greedy (GWMIN): repeatedly take the residual site maximizing w/(deg+1), then remove it and its neighbors
local search: seed with a greedy set, then apply (1,2)-swaps and additions across several randomized restarts, keeping the best conflict-free portfolio
